channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Tesla hypocrisy: Dems continue investing in Elon Musk company despite painting him as villain
item.description: As the spate of violent attacks and vandalisms against Tesla owners and workers continues, some Democrats continue to invest in the company despite their party villainizing Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin's 'sloppy' Signal leak put Democrats back on offense, Sen Warner says
item.description: Sen. Mark Warner, D-Va., says the Pentagon's "sloppy" handling of its Signal chat has put Democrats back on offense.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota union employees demand Walz halt half-time in office policy
item.description: Around 500 Minnesota state employees protested Saturday, calling on DFL Gov. Tim Walz to reverse course on a policy to require more in-office work. The governor says having more employees spend at least half their time at their workplaces will improve communication and collaboration.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin's attorney general asks the state Supreme Court to stop Musk's $1 million payments
item.description: Wisconsin’s attorney general has asked the liberal-controlled state Supreme Court to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democrats ramp up town halls in GOP districts
item.description: House Democrats are ramping up their aggressive strategy of conducting town halls in Republican-held districts, vying to exploit the GOP’s advised moratorium on the events to make inroads with frustrated voters, pick up battleground seats, and flip control of the House in next year’s midterms.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP vows to stop lone activist judges from halting Trump agenda
item.description: Republicans are bashing a federal judge's ruling blocking the deportation of suspected gang members and say they are moving to ensure such an occurrence does not happen again.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Most US Institute of Peace workers get late-night word of their mass firing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most employees at the U.S. Institute of Peace, a congressionally created and funded think tank now taken over by Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, received email notices of their mass firing, the latest step in the Trump administration's government downsizing.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top vaccine official allegedly pushed out of FDA: report
item.description: Dr. Peter Marks, who played a key role in Operation Warp Speed, was reportedly forced out of the FDA, according to the Wall Street Journal. In his purported resignation letter, Marks slams HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota food shelves worried as USDA cancels truckloads of food
item.description: This week, the U.S. Department of Agriculture canceled almost half of food shelf orders from Minnesota farmers under the federal Commodity Credit Corporation. Organizations providing nutrition programs say they are concerned about how to provide for their clientele.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Trump's election order creates much confusion before the next federal election in 2026
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to change how U.S. elections are run is creating uncertainty for state and local election officials and worries about voter confusion before the next federal election, the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump-blocking judges managed to get past Senate judiciary hawks
item.description: There is some disagreement among experts and Republicans over whether enough was done to block Democratic-nominated judges, particularly those who are now blocking the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Noem ends her first visit to Latin America in Mexico to discuss crime and migration
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — U.S. Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem landed in Mexico on Friday to cap off a tour to three Latin American nations to discuss immigration, crime and deportation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No timetable from Texas governor on filling late Democratic congressman's vacant seat
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — The race to fill a Texas congressional seat has candidates but no election date more than three weeks after Rep. Sylvester Turner’s death left a vacancy in a stronghold for Democrats, who are eager to cut into Republicans’ narrow U.S. House majority.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Major law firm reaches deal with Trump to avoid White House order even as two other firms sue
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prominent international law firm reached a deal with President Donald Trump on Friday to dedicate at least $100 million in free legal services and to review its hiring practices, averting a punishing executive order like the ones directed at nearly a half-dozen other major legal
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California governor candidate Chad Bianco says he'd abolish the state income tax if elected
item.description: Citing a desire to “restore the California Dream for all,” a Republican candidate in the 2026 race to become California’s next governor says he’d eliminate the state’s income tax if elected.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Per-pupil funding going up $2 next year unless something changes in Nevada budget
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo's proposed state budget sets per-pupil funding in 2026 at $9,416, just $2 higher than the current funding level. Going higher than that is "impracticable.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here are the international students and faculty known to be targeted by ICE
item.description: The Trump administration’s crackdown on foreign students and faculty who have voiced support for the Palestinian cause has escalated, with universities across the country seeing students either arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or having their visas revoked.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Judge blocks three agencies from disclosing troves of sensitive personal data to DOGE
item.description: A federal judge on Monday indefinitely blocked the Department of Government Efficiency's access to troves of sensitive personal data from three federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
